title: A Rapid and Reproducible Protocol for Protein Extraction from Actinomyces
author: Yalpi Karthik

steps.title: Retrieve the Actinomyces culture pellet from...
steps.instruction: Retrieve the Actinomyces culture pellet from the SCN (Suggested optimal growth measured at 600 nm OD of 0.6-0.8) broth incubated for 8-10 days at 30±2°C, 100 rpm. Culture pellet was separated using cooling centrifuge at 7000 rpm for 10 mins at 4 °C.

steps.title: Separate the culture pellet (Tube A)...
steps.instruction: Separate the culture pellet (Tube A) and spent media (Tube B) (Note: the supernatant was transferred to a fresh tube, decontaminated and discarded using WHO biosafety hazardous guidelines).

steps.title: Figure 1
steps.instruction: Figure 1. Instrumentation used for the optimal culture growth and cell separation.

steps.title: The culture pellet washed 2-3 times using 0
steps.instruction: The culture pellet washed 2-3 times using 0.05M PBS, pH 7.0 (Note: Free from chelating element), re-suspended in PBS buffer and recentrifuged at 7000 rpm for 10 mins at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and add 10...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and add 10 mL of chilled Acetone solvent to culture pellet kept for 15-30 min. Residual Acetone solvent was removed using steam of nitrogen.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 1% SDS...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 1% SDS was incubated for 2 min, yielded protein extracts of sufficient purity and concentration for electrophoresis, enzyme assays, and proteomic applications, kept under -20 °C for short term shortage and lyophilized for further use.

steps.title: The Protein was quantified using Bradford...
steps.instruction: The Protein was quantified using Bradford assay with BSA standard graph showed 224 mg per gram dry weight of cells.

steps.title: Absorbance at 595 nm = 0
steps.instruction: Absorbance at 595 nm = 0.495 (in triplicates) From regression: [Protein] = (0.495 – 0.001) ÷ 0.0022 ≈ 224 µg/mL Extract volume measured: 1 mL Protein amount = 224 µg ≈ 0.224 mg Cell dry weight used for extraction: 1 mg (0.001 g) Therefore: 0.224 mg/g = 224 mg/g

steps.title: Figure 2
steps.instruction: Figure 2. Diagrammatic representation of cell separation.

steps.title: Figure 3
steps.instruction: Figure 3. Protein characterization using 10% SDS-PAGE gel, silver nitrate staining.

steps.title: Molecular weight marker
steps.instruction: Molecular weight marker: Bands are clearly visible at ~53, 45, 35, 25, 20, 17, and 11 kDa, which serve as reference standards.

steps.title: Sample lane
steps.instruction: Sample lane: A dominant intense band is observed at the top of the gel (~45 kDa), suggesting the presence of a major protein of approximately 45 kDa. A faint band is also visible slightly lower (~17-25 kDa), which may represent a degradation product or a less abundant protein. Minimal or no other strong bands are seen, indicating that the sample is relatively homogeneous.
